violations.text: Various food items were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. refrigerated, READY-TO-EAT, TIME/TEMPERATURE CONTROL FOR SAFETY FOOD prepared and held in a FOOD ESTABLISHMENT for more than 24 hours shall be clearly marked to indicate the date or day by which the FOOD shall be consumed on the PREMISES, sold, or discarded when held at a temperature of 5ºC (41ºF) or less for a maximum of 7 days. The day of preparation shall be counted as Day 1. PIC dated food items with preparation date during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2025

Per interview with PIC and maintenance staff wastewater after mopping is disposed into the toilet. Toilets and urinals may not be used as a service sink for the disposal of mop water and similar liquid waste. At least 1 service sink or 1 curbed cleaning facility equipped with a floor drain shall be provided and conveniently located for the cleaning of mops or similar wet floor cleaning tools and for the disposal of mop water and similar liquid waste. New Violation. Correct By: 09/08/2025

-The ceiling in the food prep area is covered with fabric that is not smooth, durable, non-absorbent and easily cleanable. -Walls in food prep area are not made of smooth, durable, and easily cleanable material. Ceilings and walls must be smooth, durable, nonabsorbent, easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 09/08/2025
